title: [FIRRTL] Remove Unused Module Inputs
body:
CIRCT is currently _not_ pruning unused non-top module inputs while the Scala FIRRTL Compiler (SFC) is.

Consider the following circuit:

```scala
circuit Foo:
  module Bar:
    input a: UInt<1>
    output b: UInt<1>
    input c_unused: UInt<1>
    output d_unused: UInt<1>

    b <= a
    d_unused is invalid

  module Foo:
    input a: UInt<1>
    output b: UInt<1>

    inst bar of Bar
    bar.a <= a
    b <= bar.b

    bar.c_unused is invalid
```

CIRCT produces (`firtool Foo.fir`):
```verilog
module Bar(	// Bar.fir:2:10
  input  a, c_unused,
  output b, d_unused);

  assign b = a;	// Bar.fir:2:10
  assign d_unused = 1'h0;	// Bar.fir:2:10, :9:14
endmodule

module Foo(	// Bar.fir:11:10
  input  a,
  output b);

  wire bar_d_unused;	// Bar.fir:15:5

  Bar bar (	// Bar.fir:15:5
    .a        (a),
    .c_unused (1'h0),	// Bar.fir:19:18
    .b        (b),
    .d_unused (bar_d_unused)
  );
endmodule
```

The SFC produces:
```verilog
module Bar(
  input   a,
  output  b
);
  assign b = a;
endmodule
module Foo(
  input   a,
  output  b
);
  wire  bar_a;
  wire  bar_b;
  Bar bar (
    .a(bar_a),
    .b(bar_b)
  );
  assign b = bar_b;
  assign bar_a = a;
endmodule
```
